mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month period signals a severe economic contraction is underway. This triggers downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and increases investor risk aversion, causing negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The sharp jump in unemployment in early 2008 is a classic recessionary signal.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event during the walk-forward test period.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the federal funds rate represents the cost of capital and the opportunity cost of holding equities. A higher rate tightens financial conditions and makes bonds more attractive relative to stocks, exerting continuous downward pressure on equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard dividend discount models show that equity valuations are inversely related to the risk-free rate.
failure_condition: The beta coefficient on the FEDFUNDS level in a predictive regression for 20-day returns is not statistically significant and negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and decelerating real consumer spending signals a stagflationary environment. This erodes corporate profit margins through higher input costs while simultaneously reducing revenue, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-Year CPIAUCSL > 4% and 3-month average RSAFS growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:In mid-2008, energy price spikes are pushing CPI higher while consumer spending is plummeting.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal is not consistently greater than the IC of the individual CPIAUCSL and RSAFS signals in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: A negative trend in industrial production reflects a broad-based slowdown in the manufacturing and production sectors. This is a leading indicator of declining corporate revenues and earnings, thus predicting negative equity market returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has been contracting in early 2008, consistent with the onset of a recession.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 36-month correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In an economic cycle defined by the housing market, a sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts signals a collapse in construction, negative wealth effects, and stress in the financial system. This specific shock propagates through the economy, causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The collapse of the US housing market is the central feature of the 2007-2008 economic environment.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power (measured by Rank IC) is not significantly higher during periods of housing market stress compared to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: The 3-month change in nonfarm payrolls is a robust, timely indicator of the direction of the real economy. Positive changes signal expansion and support corporate earnings, while negative changes signal contraction, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The NBER business cycle dating committee heavily weights PAYEMS data; job losses have been consistent in 2008.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently positive and significant over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and unemployment is rising, the central bank is constrained in its ability to ease policy. This policy conflict increases uncertainty about future growth and interest rates, raising the equity risk premium and causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:In 2008, the Fed faces both high inflation and rising unemployment, limiting its policy options.
failure_condition: Equity market volatility (e.g., VIX) does not significantly increase when both trigger conditions are met, compared to when only one or neither is met.

mechanism_narrative: The official release of a negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth figure confirms a broad-based economic contraction. This acts as a powerful catalyst for investors to sell equities in the immediate aftermath, anticipating a full-blown recession.
candidate_trigger: Latest QoQ annualized GDPC1 growth is negative
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2008 GDP growth was very weak, and Q4 2007 was also revised down, confirming a sharp slowdown.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return in the week following the release of a negative GDP report is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).
